_note: Recorded flagship session: upstream rename customer_id -> cust_id. Drives the V2 demo and Seam-1 rename test deterministically without an LLM key. Tool RESULTS are produced by really running read_file/get_schema/edit_file.

turns.text: The model failed referencing a column that no longer exists upstream. Let me read the failing model.
It selects customer_id from raw_customers. Let me check the current upstream columns to confirm the drift.
Confirmed: raw_customers no longer has customer_id; it now exposes cust_id (same type). This is a clean 1:1 rename. Applying the minimal fix.

Upstream renamed the source column customer_id to cust_id, which broke the orders model's SELECT. I updated the model to reference cust_id instead. The change is a single-column rename on the failing model only; no other files touched.
